title: Envoy
content:
There clung three roses to a stem,
     Did all their hues of summer don,
     But came a wind and troubled them,
        And all were gone.

     I heard three bells in unison
     Clap out some transient heart's delight,
     Time and the hour brought silence on
        And the dark night.

     Doth not Orion even set!
     O love, love, prove true alone,
     Till youthful hearts ev'n love forget,
        Then, child, begone!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Walter De La Mare, titled "Envoy", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
